2606.27717.title: Do Speech Emphasis Models Generalize across Languages and Emotions?
2606.27717.paper_summary: Prosodic emphasis varies across languages, emotions, and speaking styles, yet existing emphasis detection models are largely trained and evaluated on monolingual neutral read speech. We introduce MMEE (Multilingual Multi-Emotion Emphasis), a corpus of 10,000 professionally recorded expressive utterances (14.13 hours) across 7 languages and 34 emotion/style categories, with three-level perceptual labels (10 annotations per sample). We benchmark two state-of-the-art architectures under monolingual, cross-lingual, multilingual, cross-emotion, cross-dataset, and data-scale settings. Monolingual models show limited zero-shot transfer, degrading across typologically distant languages, while multilingual training substantially improves robustness. Models transfer robustly between high- and low-arousal emotions; bidirectional transfer between synthetic and perceptual benchmarks suggests shared prosodic structure; and performance stays robust even at smaller training scales.
2606.27717.paper_summary_zh: 語調強調在不同語言、情感和說話風格中有所不同，然而現有的強調檢測模型主要是在單語中立朗讀語音上進行訓練和評估。我們介紹了MMEE（多語言多情感強調），這是一個包含10,000個專業錄製的表達性語句（14.13小時），涵蓋7種語言和34種情感/風格類別，並提供三級感知標籤（每個樣本10個註釋）。我們在單語、跨語言、多語言、跨情感、跨數據集和數據規模設置下基準測試了兩種最先進的架構。單語模型顯示出有限的零樣本轉移，在類型學上相距遙遠的語言中表現下降，而多語言訓練顯著提高了穩健性。模型在高喚起和低喚起情感之間穩健轉移；合成和感知基準之間的雙向轉移表明共享的語調結構；即使在較小的訓練規模下，性能仍然保持穩健。
2606.27717.author: Megan Wei et.al.
2606.27717.authors: Megan Wei, Deepali Aneja, Jiaqi Su, Yunyun Wang, Haonan Chen, Zeyu Jin
